title: Incorrect Trie Insertion Causing Duplicate Node Creation

statement.markdown: Implement a solution to insert words into a trie. Ensure that each word is stored exactly once and that insertion is performed efficiently. The solution should handle large datasets. The trie can contain at most 10,000 characters.
statement.inputFormat: The input will contain words to be inserted into the trie, one word per line.
statement.outputFormat: The solution should print the updated trie after all words have been inserted.
statement.notes: The solution should minimize the number of memory allocations.

examples.output: Updated trie after all words have been inserted
examples.explanation: The solution should create a trie containing 'hello', 'world', and 'helloworld'.

hints.text: Use a depth-first search approach to insert words into the trie.
Consider handling duplicate words by checking if a word is already present in the trie before attempting to insert it.

editorial.title: Correct Approach to Trie Insertion
editorial.intuition: The solution should use a trie data structure to efficiently store words. When inserting a word, the solution should traverse the trie and create new nodes as necessary. If a word is already present in the trie, the solution should handle it by updating the existing nodes.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive approach: insert each word into the trie by iterating over each character and creating new nodes as necessary.
editorial.betterSolution: Improved approach: use a hash map to store the existing words in the trie and update the nodes accordingly.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal approach: use a trie data structure and leverage its existing functionality to efficiently insert words.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because it ensures that each word is stored exactly once in the trie and that insertion is performed efficiently.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: insert the words 'hello', 'world', and 'helloworld' into the trie and verify that the resulting trie contains the correct nodes.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class TrieNode { ... }; class Solution { public: void insert(string word) { ... } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class TrieNode { ... }; class Solution { public void insert(String word) { ... } };
buggyStarterCode.python: class TrieNode: ...; class Solution: def insert(self, word: str) -> None: ...
buggyStarterCode.javascript: class TrieNode { ... }; class Solution { public insert(word: string): void { ... } }
